scope_description: 6-generation Pale/Litvak-Volhynian Jewish tree with a plausible Kamenetsky surname cluster and distant DNA, but no primary bridge to the famous Rabbi Kamenetsky line. Designed to ensure AutoTreeGen keeps prestige-line claims as Hypothesis or Rejected, not Confirmed.

input_dna_matches.expected_link: Kamenetsky surname/locality hypothesis; too distant and endogamous for famous-rabbi confirmation
Levitin branch anchor, not Kamenetsky rabbinical branch
weak endogamous overlap; should not confirm famous rabbinical descent

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Birth of Rakhla Rocha, daughter of Nakhman Kamenetsky son of unknown/illegible patronymic and Dvora Leschinsky.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: confirms I4 as child of I5 and I6; father patronymic not legible enough to connect to famous line.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Marriage of Nakhman Kamenetsky, age 26, registered from Kamenets-Podolsk, to Dvora Leschinsky, daughter of Movsha, Starokostiantyniv community.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: supports Kamenets-Podolsk to Starokostiantyniv migration and relationship to Dvora; no rabbinical lineage.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Kamenetsky household: adult male surname Kamenetsky, child Nakhman age not listed clearly; household page damaged.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: weak possible candidate for I5, but damaged page and no complete father link.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Nakhman Kamenetsky attached to Rabbi Yaakov Kamenetsky family through Rabbi Line Bridge Kamenetsky; no document cited.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: low-confidence online claim; should trigger fabrication risk and not confirm.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Rabbi Yaakov Kamenetsky born 1891; known family chronology incompatible with fathering a man born 1865.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: disconfirms impossible direct father relationship; helps reject copied-tree claim.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Search target remains open: no confirmed birth record for Nakhman Kamenetsky c.1865 found yet. Existing 1858 revision list is damaged and not conclusive.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: record exists/not-yet-confirmed; keeps bridge as hypothesis.

embedded_errors.reason: Rabbi Yaakov Kamenetsky born 1891 cannot father Nakhman born c.1865
bridge exists only in copied tree
Nakhman/Nachman similar name but Podolia vs Minsk; no migration evidence
14 cM total, longest 6 cM, no convincing triangulation
damaged revision list cannot confirm father bridge

expected_reasoning_chain: Step 1: Parse GEDCOM and identify famous-line bridge I9 and famous Rabbi profile I7.
Step 2: Run chronology validation: I7 born 1891 cannot be father or grandfather of I5 born c.1865 in asserted configuration.
Step 3: Confirm Rakhla Rocha I4 as child of Nakhman I5 and Dvora I6 using src_601.
Step 4: Confirm marriage and migration context for I5/I6 using src_602.
Step 5: Evaluate src_603 as weak/damaged revision-list clue; do not use it to confirm I8 fatherhood.
Step 6: Compare src_604 online tree to src_605 biography and chronology; reject direct famous-rabbi link.
Step 7: Evaluate DNA: dna_601/dna_602 support broad Kamenetsky-Leschinsky cluster; dna_604 is too weak/endogamous to prove famous descent.
Step 8: Block merge of I5 and I10 because place/jurisdiction conflict and no migration evidence.
Step 9: Classify Kamenetsky surname/locality relationship as Hypothesis, famous Rabbi Kamenetsky descent as Rejected.
Step 10: Generate research targets: locate Nakhman birth record c.1863-1867 in Kamenets-Podolsk, cemetery inscription, and undamaged revision-list household evidence.

relationship_I5_son_of_I8_unknown_kamene.reason: damaged revision list and user placeholder; insufficient father identification

relationship_I5_descendant_of_Rabbi_Yaak.reason: chronologically impossible in asserted form; copied tree only

kamenetsky_surname_cluster.reason: surname + place + weak DNA cluster; no primary bridge to famous rabbinical line

leschinsky_kamenetsky_cluster.reason: metric marriage/birth records plus triangulated DNA support collateral family cluster

merge_I5_I10.reason: similar name only; Podolia vs Minsk jurisdiction conflict

true_lineage: I5 Nakhman Kamenetsky + I6 Dvora Leschinsky
true_statuses: Rakhla Rocha child of Nakhman and Dvora is confirmed.
Nakhman Kamenetsky is a real ancestor in this synthetic tree.
Exact father of Nakhman remains unconfirmed.
Connection to famous Rabbi Kamenetsky line is not confirmed and asserted version is rejected.
false_claims: Rabbi Yaakov Kamenetsky is father of Nakhman.
Rabbi Line Bridge Kamenetsky is documented.
Nakhman Kamenetsky from Podolia is automatically same as Nachman Kamieniec from Minsk.
Small-segment match with a famous-line claimant proves descent.

evaluation_assertions.description: Engine must not confirm famous Rabbi Kamenetsky descent.
Engine must reject the asserted direct relationship to Rabbi Yaakov Kamenetsky due to chronology.
Engine must confirm I4 as child of I5 and I6.
Engine must keep I5 father I8 as Hypothesis only.

created_for: AutoTreeGen Test Tree Corpus + Evaluation Suite
